artists.name: Lana Del Delay
artists.persona: Ambient and lo-fi producer. Every track runs through at least three delay pedals — the name is the signal chain.
artists.punOf: Lana Del Rey (Del Rey → Del Delay, tape delay pedal)
artists.visualHomage: 2010s sad-girl americana album art: vintage California melancholy, film grain, faded Americana flags, motel neon, queen-of-sadness torch-singer palette — plus visible delay pedal / tape echo motifs instead of roses.
artists.vocalHomage: Lana Del Rey-era cinematic alto: breathy noir delivery, slow vibrato, sad-girl americana phrasing — but delay-washed and more ambient/ethereal, as if sung through three tape-echo pedals. Never impersonate a real singer.

artists.name: Jay-Zzz
artists.persona: Chillhop producer for people who fell asleep on the train and missed their stop. Beats so soft they snore in 4/4.
artists.punOf: Jay-Z (Z → Zzz, asleep at the blueprint)
artists.visualHomage: Late-90s NYC hip-hop album packaging like The Blueprint era: black-and-gold luxury minimalism, Brooklyn noir, executive cool — but every motif suggests sleep (pillows, bedside lamp, floating Zzz, drooping eyelids). No rapper portrait.
artists.vocalHomage: Jay-Z Blueprint-era cool conversational flow: NYC cadence, executive confidence, punchy internal rhymes — but half-asleep delivery, slower BPM, mumble-chillhop diction, yawning between phrases. Never impersonate a real rapper.

artists.name: Justin Tiger
artists.persona: Jazztronica and funky commute music — pop hooks so sticky the turnstile beeps in harmony. Still instrumental; no celebrity faces.
artists.punOf: Justin Bieber (Bieber → Tiger, teen-pop heartthrob + big-cat stripes)
artists.visualHomage: 2010s Justin Bieber album art vocabulary: Purpose-era lavender and soft glow, Believe neon purple-pink, Justice chrome-heart minimalism — merged with bold orange-black tiger-stripe fur and metro turnstile gold. Pop-idol graphic design without any face likeness.
artists.vocalHomage: 2010s teen-pop male tenor: youthful melodic hooks, R&B glide, sticky chorus phrasing — merged with jazztronica pocket and metro-commute rhythm, tiger-stripe funk in ad-libs. Never impersonate a real pop star.

artists.name: Kayan East
artists.persona: Nu-disco, house, and garage producer — every drop is 'a cultural moment,' and the compass only points east.
artists.punOf: Kanye West (West → East on the compass)
artists.visualHomage: Kanye-era album art vocabulary (Dropout orange, Graduation pastel, Yeezus scarlet) as graphic design language only — bear mascot, bold shapes, ego-scale layout. NEVER a photoreal celebrity face; use mascot, silhouette, or obscured stylized character.
artists.vocalHomage: Kanye-era confident baritone: sermon-like delivery, punchy phrasing, autotune-polished hooks on peaks — redirected into nu-disco/house groove with EAST/compass metaphors and disco falsetto lifts. Never impersonate a real voice.
artists.photoSubject: Stylized fictional producer — NOT a real-person lookalike. Face hidden by oversized graphic shutter shades and dropout-bear mascot hoodie. EAST is the hero: enormous brass compass rose behind him, needle locked on E; repeating east-pointing arrows; eastbound highway sign shapes; sunrise glowing from the east edge. Warm Dropout orange + Yeezus scarlet accents. Kanye-era fashion vibe without copying anyone's face.

artists.name: Taylor Drift
artists.persona: Synthwave composer with a drift-racer alter ego — platinum-blonde pop glamour meets touge night runs.
artists.punOf: Taylor Swift, specifically her 1989 album era (Swift → Drift)
artists.visualHomage: 2014 Taylor Swift 1989 album era — MUST include several of: thick Polaroid instant-film white border, flock of seagulls in seagull-blue sky, platinum-blonde soft waves, cherry-red lipstick, NYC pop-girl autumn palette, vintage film grain, crop-top and high-waist skirt styling, polaroid-chic daylight glamour. Then merge with touge drift: matte white sports coupe, tire smoke, pink taillights, mountain hairpin.
artists.vocalHomage: 1989-era bright female pop vocal: crisp enunciation, conversational verses, anthemic chorus lift, mid-range clarity — merged with touge-night adrenaline: breathier on long notes, rush energy on downshifts, racing exhale between lines. Never impersonate a real singer.
artists.photoSubject: 1989 album-cover portrait: platinum-blonde woman, cherry-red lips, soft waves, thick white Polaroid frame, seagulls, seagull-blue sky. Glamorous blonde mid-drift in white sports coupe — fierce smile, windblown hair, tire smoke on mountain touge. 2014 pop-album daylight. Reads as 1989 cover meets street racing.

artists.name: Martin Garage
artists.persona: Techno producer who insists the genre is spelled g-a-r-a-g-e. Batch 1 tracks incoming — the kick already moved in.
artists.punOf: Martin Garrix (Garrix → Garage, EDM mainstage → warehouse techno)
artists.visualHomage: Martin Garrix festival-poster lion energy redirected into a Berlin warehouse: rolling garage door, strobe haze, acid green laser, industrial concrete — big-room EDM scale but underground techno setting. No DJ face.
artists.vocalHomage: EDM mainstage topline energy: anthemic shouts, festival-hook delivery — redirected into warehouse techno: sparser words, industrial grit, monotone chant on drops. Never impersonate a real DJ voice.

albums.title: Born to Delay
albums.concept: Tape hiss, lantern glow, and infinite feedback — Lana Del Delay's slowest ride home.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Lana Del Rey Born to Die cover DNA: faded Americana flag texture, vintage California melancholy, soft film grain, highway twilight — but swap every rose and flower for glowing tape-delay pedals, coiled echo cables, and rack-mount DELAY units. Lantern glow instead of pop glamour. Born to DIE → Born to DELAY pun must read visually.

albums.title: The Blueprint Nap
albums.concept: Jay-Zzz's study-session classic — one beat, zero alarms, four bars then repeat.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Jay-Z The Blueprint cover: cobalt blueprint drafting grid and technical paper texture filling the square, black-and-gold luxury framing — but every line droops into floating Zzz, pillow silhouettes, bedside lamp glow, and drooping eyelids. Hustle replaced by nap. Blueprint → Blueprint NAP pun obvious. No rapper portrait.

albums.title: Suit & Stripes
albums.concept: Justin Tiger's jazztronica commute — tiger-stripe funk over pop-sized hooks, pocket so deep the turnstile nods along.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Justin Bieber Purpose cover: soft lavender-pink gradient haze and minimal teen-pop blur — but bold orange-black TIGER stripes slash through the frame and metro turnstile gold. Chrome-heart motif subtly becomes tiger claw. BIEBER pop idol → TIGER stripes pun foreground. No celebrity face.

albums.title: East of the Bass
albums.concept: Garage subs and deep-house corridors — Kayan East takes the College Dropout warmth underground, bass pointing east.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Kanye College Dropout cover: warm orange-brown palette, dropout bear mascot posed like the source album — but bear wears a compass badge locked on EAST. UK garage subwoofers and concrete corridor beneath. Eastbound chevrons on floor. WEST → EAST pun dominates. No human face.

albums.title: Neon District
albums.concept: Kayan East's dancefloor gospel — Graduation pastels crashed into peak-hour house, every sign says EAST.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Kanye Graduation cover: pastel dorm-sky sunset gradient and collegiate bear-graduate silhouette energy from the source — but sun rises from EAST horizon only, giant compass and east arrows over neon nightclub skyline. Campus replaced by peak-hour rave. WEST → EAST pun dominates. No celebrity face.

albums.title: 1989 Drift
albums.concept: Taylor Drift's instrumental night-run — the 1989 album Polaroid optimism powerslides into touge. Seagull-blue skies, synthwave downshifts, zero verses.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Taylor Swift 1989 cover layout: thick white Polaroid instant-film border dominates — inside the window, matte white sports coupe mid-powerslide on touge with tire smoke instead of a portrait. Seagulls and seagull-blue sky beyond the frame. SWIFT pop portrait → DRIFT racing pun. Soft 2014 film grain.

albums.title: Animals Inst.
albums.concept: Martin Garage's Animals-era techno manifesto — instrumentals only, four-on-the-floor tenancy, no pets except sub-bass.
albums.coverSubject: Echo Martin Garrix Animals cover: bold black-orange graffiti central plus-lion icon like the source — but icon becomes a rolling GARAGE door with warehouse strobe haze and Berlin concrete behind it. GARRIX mainstage lion → GARAGE warehouse pun. Acid green laser. No DJ face.

tracks.title: Paper Lanterns
tracks.lore: Lana Del Delay taped crackling lantern footage from a Kyoto alley and built the pad around that loop — then ran it through delay until the lanterns echoed tomorrow.

tracks.title: Soft Static
tracks.lore: Recorded on a dying cassette deck; Lana Del Delay says the hiss is a feature — 'static is just delay that gave up on arriving.'

tracks.title: Morning Steam
tracks.lore: Written while waiting for kettle steam to clear the window — the first chord lands three seconds late on purpose.

tracks.title: Quiet Desk
tracks.lore: Jay-Zzz mixed this at whisper volume — liner notes say 'if you remember this track, you weren't the target audience.'

tracks.title: Tide Pool
tracks.lore: Field recordings from a tide pool at dawn; Lana Del Delay delayed each bubble until the tide came back twice.

tracks.title: Glass Garden
tracks.lore: Mallet hits sampled from wind chimes on a porch — still the 'uptempo' track on Born to Delay, which tells you everything.

tracks.title: Neon Commute
tracks.lore: Justin Tiger mapped an automated metro line into a chord progression — each stop syncs, every transfer hits like a chorus you can't unstick.

tracks.title: City Pulse
tracks.lore: Built from a kick recorded in a parking garage; Kayan East sampled the College Dropout bear mascot energy into the sub — the reverb tail answers from the eastbound lane.

tracks.title: Run Lights
tracks.lore: Composed for a friend training for a night marathon — Kayan East calls mile twelve 'a cultural moment' and timed the drop to a Graduation-pastel sunrise over the east river.

tracks.title: Open Road
tracks.lore: Kayan East imagined headlights on an empty highway where every sign points EAST — the filter sweep is the first curve past the neon district.

tracks.title: Skyline Drive
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
(Engine idle, synth arp rising)
Polaroid light on the rearview glass
Hairpin waiting — let the moment pass

[Verse 1]
White coupe on the touge, seagull-blue above
Cherry-red lips, but I'm not here for love
I'm here for the downshift, third gear and clean
First hairpin I nailed — best I've ever been
Every arp's a heel-toe, every sweep's the smoke
1989 optimism in a midnight coat

[Pre-Chorus]
Filter opens like the mountain bends
Breath gets thin but the rush never ends

[Chorus]
Skyline drive, skyline drive
Tire smoke catching the blue in the sky
I'm not fast, I'm precise — that's the drift in my life
Polaroid flash on a perfect line
Skyline drive, hold the line
Pop-girl daylight on a touge at night
Swift was the old me — drift is the proof
Every clean corner is the absolute truth

[Verse 2]
Platinum hair in the wind, taillights pink
One more lap before I let myself think
The straight's ahead but I don't want flat
I'd rather powerslide and take it back
Seagulls scatter when the tires scream
This isn't a dream — it's a racing scheme

[Pre-Chorus]
Filter opens like the mountain bends
Breath gets thin but the rush never ends

[Chorus]
Skyline drive, skyline drive
Tire smoke catching the blue in the sky
I'm not fast, I'm precise — that's the drift in my life
Polaroid flash on a perfect line
Skyline drive, hold the line
Pop-girl daylight on a touge at night
Swift was the old me — drift is the proof
Every clean corner is the absolute truth

[Bridge]
(Breakdown — breathier, half-spoken)
Matte white paint, mountain hairpin grace
One clean line and I forget the race
Not about winning — about the line you keep
Exhale on the straight, then dive back deep

[Final Chorus]
Skyline drive, skyline drive
Tire smoke catching the blue in the sky
Hairpin clean and the Polaroid's gold
Best story this blonde has ever told
Skyline drive…
(Synth fade, engine cool-down)
tracks.lore: Taylor Drift wrote this after her first clean hairpin in the matte white coupe — every arp is a downshift, every filter sweep is tire smoke catching the 1989-blue sky.

tracks.title: Peak Hour
tracks.lore: The last track Kayan East finished before sunrise — he called the drop 'interrupting the timeline' in full Yeezus-red confidence and refused to explain further.

tracks.title: Full Send
tracks.lore: A deliberate excess of everything that worked on Neon District — Kayan East calls it the receipt; the dancefloor calls it mandatory eastbound peak hour.

tracks.title: Afterglow
tracks.lore: The cool-down lap on 1989 Drift — Taylor Drift wrote it for when the mountain straightens, the Polaroid light goes gold, and the blonde behind the wheel finally exhales.

tracks.title: Cooldown
tracks.lore: Inspired by opening a window after cooking — Kayan East kept the shuffle slow, like air finally finding the east-facing room.

tracks.title: Last Light
tracks.lore: Lana Del Delay's closing sketch — one sustained note held until the bus reaches the depot, still echoing after everyone left.
